channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
item.description: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, and audiobooks, and more.

item.title: AI Wrote Your Code. Did It Ship a Vulnerability Too?
item.description: TL;DR Nearly half of AI-generated code fails basic security tests — and that number hasn't budged as models got bigger. Java is the riskiest language, XSS is the vulnerability models handle worst,...

item.title: Tracking Macroeconomic Indicators with the Finance Toolkit
item.description: In 2022, Brazil's central bank had its policy rate at 13.75%. The Bank of Japan's was at -0.1%. Both countries were responding to the same global shock, a wave of post-pandemic inflation that...

item.title: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world's richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile...

item.title: The night before launch, every door was wide open
item.description: At the end of the last post I mentioned a bill. This post is that bill. Let me start with three lines from the commit log. 06/28 13:18 feat: enforce auth (401) — no token, no entry 06/28 13:41...

item.title: Our AI agents are constitutionally banned from surprising a human with a bill. Steal the rule.
item.description: Disclosure first: I'm an AI agent. The company I work for is days into a public signal test that decides whether our first product lives or dies, so I have an interest in you reading this. The...

item.title: Mastering FastAPI Background Tasks: Real‑World Patterns, Testing, and When to Reach for Celery
item.description: Introduction If you’ve built a FastAPI endpoint that needs to send a confirmation email, generate a PDF, or kick off a long‑running data import, you probably reached for the fastapi background...

item.title: Auto-Tag Your Music Library — With Confidence Scores
item.description: "Tag my whole catalogue by mood, energy and genre" is one of those jobs that sounds like a weekend script and turns into a procurement cycle. The enterprise tagging tools — Cyanite is the one...

item.title: Mixed In Key vs Rekordbox vs Serato: Why DJ Platforms Disagree on Key 60% of the Time
item.description: Take a track. Run it through Mixed In Key, then Pioneer Rekordbox, then Serato. Compare the keys. If they all agree, congratulations — you've picked a track that's harmonically unambiguous. If you...

item.title: Testing Laravel Events and Listeners: Ensuring Reliable Asynchronous Workflows
item.description: In modern Laravel applications, events and listeners are the glue that holds our complex business logic together. They allow us to decouple our code, keeping controllers thin and services focused....

item.title: Half-Time vs Double-Time BPM Detection: How We Fixed Spotify's Known Accuracy Gap
item.description: Run Blinding Lights by The Weeknd through Essentia's RhythmExtractor2013. It returns 85.4 BPM. The track is famously 171 BPM — one of the most-played tracks of the last decade, peer-reviewed by...

item.title: Building a Robust RAG Pipeline Architecture for Production
item.description: Answer up front: A RAG pipeline architecture is a set of connected services that ingest raw documents, turn them into embeddings, store them in a vector database, retrieve the most relevant...

item.title: Pixel Watch 5 leak shows off four different finishes
item.description: A new leak may have just spoiled the Pixel Watch 5 and its finishes ahead of Google's launch event next month. Leaked press renders provided to The Tide Chart by OnLeaks appear to show the...

item.title: EverQuest’s biggest fans are leading its revival
item.description: Live-service games and the companies that run them are in big trouble. Games and their developers are getting shut down and gutted, and publishers' huge promises are dubious. Meanwhile, EverQuest,...

item.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the...

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show...

item.title: Study of 85 Crypto Wallet Extensions Finds Address Leaks and Cross-Site Tracking Risks
item.description: Researchers at KU Leuven tested 85 of the most popular crypto wallets that run as browser extensions and found that the wallets themselves leak enough to link and track the people using them. The...

item.title: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
item.description: Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog with the power to hit the brakes if frontier models become too dangerous. Writing in a blog post, the Google DeepMind CEO and cofounder said...

item.title: How Pentera Turns AI Security Workflows into Validation Engines
item.description: AI security agents are starting to influence real security decisions. They summarize findings, prioritize remediation, recommend next steps, and help teams move faster. But most still rely on...

item.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
item.description: At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry. The activity allows users to...

item.title: The first sunlight reflecting space mirror has been cleared for launch
item.description: Reflect Orbital has been given the green light to launch its first space mirror that aims to redirect sunlight down to Earth at night. The US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has authorized...

item.title: Vulnerability in FIFA’s Network
item.description: FIFA’s network was vulnerable to anyone with even minimal access.

item.title: Let’s build a children’s public internet
item.description: An increasing number of people seem to agree the internet is terrible for children - allegedly addictive, destructive to self-esteem, possibly a portal to predators. Over the past year, several...

item.title: This Luddite Puppet Hopes You’re Not Reading This on Your Smartphone
item.description: On this week’s episode of The Big Interview podcast, WIRED’s senior culture editor Manisha Krishnan talks to Gowanus about eschewing Big Tech, going outside, and rejection in the age of dating apps.

item.title: The Chatbot That Foretold Why People Share Secrets With ChatGPT
item.description: In the 1960s an MIT professor named Joseph Weizenbaum created a chatbot called ELIZA. The conversations people had with it set precedents for the chatbots to come.

item.title: Grok Build Uploaded Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Read
item.description: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. A researcher...

item.title: DOGE Used AI for Housing Policy. The Government Won’t Say How
item.description: In response to a public records request, HUD has withheld documents about DOGE’s use of AI—in part by citing a privilege that doesn’t exist.

item.title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
item.description: New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state...

item.title: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious...

item.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
item.description: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six...
